people.name: Charagui
people.blurb: Capitanejo who defeated the San Diego column at Jacum in May 1837, holding the field against 25 soldiers and 50 auxiliaries.

people.blurb: Ex-alcalde of Mission San José who led the largest neophyte military resistance of the Mexican period from fortified river positions on the Stanislaus, 1829.

people.blurb: Tongva woman who led the 1785 revolt at Mission San Gabriel.

people.name: Talholostl
people.blurb: Captain killed in the 1798 punitive battle near San Juan Bautista. The reading of his name in the manuscript is uncertain.

people.name: Valli-Ela
people.blurb: Chief who requested a Russian flag, navigating between empires on his own account.

people.name: Guirmenes
people.blurb: The Bodega-area man whose intelligence the Spanish valued enough to order that he be kept happy.

people.name: Palma
people.blurb: Quechan leader whose alliance Spain won and then lost; the withheld gifts to Palma are among the named causes of the 1781 Yuma revolt.

nations.blurb: The people on whose land Colony Ross was built; Hagemeister's treaty with them was Russia's answer to Spain's claim.

nations.blurb: The peoples of the San Diego frontier, from the 1775 destruction of the mission to the Jacum battle of 1837 — on both sides of the presidio's wars.

nations.name: Ohlone (Costanoan)
nations.blurb: The San Francisco peninsula, the East Bay, and the Monterey Bay country; the peoples of Missions Dolores, Santa Clara, San José, and Carmel. Shown here holding both shores of Carquinez Strait through the Karkin: a coastal strip about two miles deep on the north shore, from Crockett past Benicia. An editorial departure from the Handbook plate, which ends Costanoan territory at the south shore.

nations.name: Owens Valley Paiute
nations.blurb: A Native nation of California; territory shown on the Native California map.

nations.name: Patwin
nations.blurb: The lower west side of the Sacramento Valley; the Suisun people of the 1810–17 campaigns spoke a Patwin language.

nations.name: Pomo
nations.blurb: The Russian River country and Clear Lake; several distinct languages share the name. The Kashaya, on whose coast Fort Ross was built, are the southwestern Pomo people.

nations.name: Salinan
nations.blurb: The upper Salinas River country; drawn into Missions San Antonio and San Miguel.

nations.name: Serrano
nations.blurb: The San Bernardino mountains and the desert beyond Cajon Pass.

nations.name: Shasta
nations.blurb: The Shasta Valley and upper Klamath country.

nations.name: Southern Valley Yokuts
nations.blurb: A Native nation of California; territory shown on the Native California map.

nations.name: Tataviam
nations.blurb: The upper Santa Clara River, north of the San Fernando Valley.

nations.name: Tolowa
nations.blurb: The Smith River country in the far northwest corner.

nations.name: Tongva (Gabrielino)
nations.blurb: The Los Angeles basin and the southern Channel Islands; the people of Mission San Gabriel and of Toypurina's 1785 revolt.
